items.title: IOI Corp's Q3 earnings rise 8.0pct to RM283.3mil on lower revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Corp Bhd posted an eight per cent rise in net profit for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, to RM283.3 million from RM262.3 million a year ago. IOI' Corp's revenue, however, dropped 2.1 per cent to RM2.67 billion from RM2.73 billion a year earlier, its Bursa Malaysia filing showed. For the nine-month period, its net profit grew 8.9 per cent to RM1.18 billion from RM1.08 billion a year earlier, while revenue rose 4.3 per cent to RM8.73 billion against RM8.37 billion previously.

items.title: Innovation is no longer the CEO's job alone
items.summary: Malaysia has long spoken about becoming an innovation-driven nation. Yet in many organisations, innovation still sits within a strategy unit, research department, or the minds of senior executives. Most importantly, it democratises innovation.

items.title: Strong winds, rough seas forecast for Sabah, Labuan and northern Sarawak from May 30 to June 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast significant weather conditions in western Sabah, the Federal Territory of Labuan and northern Sarawak following the convergence of strong westerly winds expected from May 30 to June 1. It said the conditions could result in strong winds and rough seas, as well as the formation of squall lines expected to bring wetter and windier conditions over the affected areas and waters. “In this regard, MetMalaysia has issued a strong winds and rough seas warning for the South China Sea and the northern Straits of Melaka waters, effective until June 1,” it said in a statement today.

items.title: WTK's unit to dispose of shares in Biogrow City Plantations to Rimbun Temasek
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak-based diversified group WTK Holdings Bhd's units Biogrow City Sdn Bhd (BCSB) and Bioworld Synergies Sdn Bhd (BSSB) have proposed to sell their interest and preference shares in Biogrow City Plantations Sdn Bhd (BCPSB) to Rimbun Temasek Sdn Bhd for RM90 million. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, it said the proposed disposals comprise BCSB's disposal of 8.5 million ordinary shares, representing an 85 per cent equity interest in BCPSB, for RM65.6 million, and BSSB's disposal of 24.4 million redeemable preference shares in BCPSB for RM24.4 million. WTK executive director Francis Lai said the proposed disposals provide the group with an opportunity to unlock value from underperforming assets that are geographically distant from its main plantation clusters, as plantations remain a key business segment for WTK.

items.title: IJM Corp unit to sell Sabah property to Econsave-linked company for RM47.5mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Corporation Bhd's (IJM Corp) indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, IJM Properties Sdn Bhd, has signed a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) to dispose of a piece of land together with a single-storey building in Sandakan, Sabah, to Coupang Sdn Bhd for RM47.5 million in cash. Coupang is the asset-holding company of Econsave Cash & Carry Sdn Bhd (Econsave Group). IJM Corp also said the proceeds from the proposed disposal are intended primarily for working capital requirements, with RM46 million earmarked for the purpose within 24 months of the disposal's completion.

items.title: Johor BN reps confirm receiving notice for June 22 sitting
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Several Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen have confirmed receiving notices to attend a state assembly sitting scheduled for June 22. The assemblymen disclosed this after attending the Johor BN meeting at the Johor Umno Liaison Committee office here today. Speculation regarding a Johor state election has intensified following a leaked message about a special state assembly sitting allegedly scheduled for June 24 to dissolve the state assembly.

items.title: Wan Saiful: Hamzah has moved on, never plotted to split Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tasek Gelugor member of parliament Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan defended former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin against claims that he sought to divide the party. He dismissed allegations by Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, a former political secretary to Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, who said Hamzah had used Pas, Umno and the royal institution to split Bersatu. "Hamzah has moved on from Bersatu.

items.title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on renewed risk appetite
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended the week higher against the US dollar on Friday, supported by renewed risk appetite following reports of a possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route. At 6pm, the local currency rose to 3.9625/9670 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 3.9770/9805. Meanwhile, the local currency traded mostly higher against regional peers.

items.title: Japan spent US$73bil in yen interventions
items.summary: TOKYO: The Bank of Japan spent around 11.7 trillion yen (US$73 billion) in the past month to prop up the yen, official government data showed on Friday. The interventions reportedly began on April 30 when the Japanese currency weakened to near 160 yen per dollar, the lowest in almost two years. Japanese media reported on May 8 that the bank had by then spent 10 trillion yen propping up the currency.

items.title: Guan Chong's net profit rose 30pct to RM123.58mil in Q1
items.summary: In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the higher earnings posted during the quarter was due to lower finance costs arising from lower borrowing. However, the company's revenue slipped 39.1 per cent to RM2.62 billion in Q1 FY26 from RM4.31 billion in the same period last year dragged down by lower selling price for cocoa products. Looking ahead, the company said cocoa prices are expected to remain relatively stable following the significant correction from last year's highs.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 29, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Friday, extending its losses despite easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, although overall market breadth remained positive. Among the most actively traded counters at the close were Axiata Bhd, YTL Corp Bhd and Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Inta Bina bags RM227.08mil mixed commercial development contract in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Inta Bina Group Bhd has secured a RM227.08 million contract from Ancubic PJ Sdn Bhd for the proposed main building and infrastructure works for a mixed commercial development in Klang, Selangor. Following the contract award, Inta Bina's unfilled order book stands at approximately RM1.7 billion, including the newly secured project. Inta Bina managing director Paul Lim said the contract strengthens the group's order book and reinforces its presence in the Klang Valley construction market.

items.title: Malaysia, Saudi advance cooperation in security and enforcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Saudi Arabia are deepening security and enforcement ties, with both countries finalising key areas of cooperation ranging from border control to counter-terrorism under the Saudi-Malaysia Coordination Council (SMCC). Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said progress on the matters was discussed during a meeting with Saudi Arabia's Interior Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Nayef bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud after he performed the haj pilgrimage this year. "Through the SMCC Security Cluster, Malaysia and Saudi Arabia have finalised seven key areas of cooperation covering border security, counter-terrorism, cyber security, immigration, forensics, search and rescue operations, and efforts to combat narcotics crimes," he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Anwar calls for responsible reporting in age of information overload
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has stressed the importance of responsible reporting in an era of information overload and borderless news dissemination to ensure facts are conveyed accurately, ethically and without undermining social harmony. In a Facebook post marking the National Journalists’ Day (Hawana) today, Anwar said the role of the media extends beyond reporting the news, carrying instead a major responsibility to uphold truth with integrity, credibility, courage and accountability. “Today, we commemorate National Journalists’ Day as a tribute to journalists and media practitioners who have long served as the voice of the people and as one of the key pillars strengthening the nation’s democracy.

items.title: Man found dead in locked car outside Penang ice cream shop
items.summary: Timur Laut District Deputy Police Chief Supt Lim Swee Sake said police received information on the incident from members of the public at 11.47am. “Investigations on the victim’s body and the surrounding area found no elements of foul play,” he said in a statement today. Lim said police estimated that the victim had died about five days before he was discovered.

items.title: Johor assemblyman dismisses election talk over one-day sitting
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation surrounding a possible dissolution of the Johor state assembly following the announcement of a one-day sitting on June 22 should be stopped to avoid unnecessary tension and political perception. DAP Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim said the brief state assembly sitting was not unprecedented and should not automatically be interpreted as a precursor to fresh state elections. she said.

items.title: Ampang, Sri Petaling LRT line runs on modified schedule after Chan Sow Lin derailment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Light Rail Transit (LRT) stations along the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line continued to operate today on a modified service schedule following the train derailment near Chan Sow Lin Station yesterday. A Bernama survey at several stations along the line, including from Cheras Station to Pudu Station, found that operations were running as usual with ticket counters open to the public. A passenger, Arif Norazlan, 21, said his journey from Cheras Station to Pudu Station went smoothly thanks to the assistance and guidance provided by Rapid Rail staff.

items.title: Finas: Screenings of local film 'Konspirasi' halted at publisher's request
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The nationwide screening of local film Konspirasi has been halted since May 28 at the request of its publisher, the National Film Development Corporation Malaysia (Finas) said. Finas said it was informed of the matter today by the Malaysian Association of Film Exhibitors (MAFE) and distributor Media Prima Bhd. The film began screening on May 21 across 120 cinema screens nationwide.

items.title: Cabnet unit bags RM38.7mil data centre containment system contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cabnet Holdings Bhd's (CHB) wholly-owned subsidiary, Cabnet Systems (M) Sdn Bhd (CSM), has secured a RM38.68 million contract from Unique Arena Sdn Bhd for the supply and installation of a data centre containment system in Selangor. "The contract works are expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings and net assets per share over the duration of the contract works. "The contract works will not have any impact on the share capital and shareholding structure of the company," CHB said.

items.title: Ewon’s Upko applies to join GRS, moves to deepen Sabah coalition ties
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 29 — The United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) is officially asking to become a component party of the governing Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) coalition. “The ‘Sabah First’ vision championed by Upko is aligned with GRS’ struggle through the Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 roadmap, which must be strengthened through closer cooperation and unity among Sabah local parties,” he added. GRS currently has five component parties — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah (Gagasan Rakyat/PGRS), Parti Bersatu Sabah (PBS), Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah (PHRS), Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) and Parti Cinta Sabah (PCS) after the departure of several parties following the state election.

items.title: TNB issues RM4bil sustainability sukuk wakalah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has issued RM4 billion worth of Sustainability Sukuk Wakalah under its Sukuk Wakalah Programme. "The tranches include: RM450 million with a seven-year tenure carrying a periodic distribution rate of 3.81 per cent per annum; RM300 million with a 10-year tenure at 3.93 per cent; RM850 million with a 15-year tenure at 4.19 per cent; RM1.05 billion with a 20-year tenure at 4.31 per cent; and RM1.35 billion with a 25-year tenure at 4.37 per cent," said TNB. Maybank Investment Bank Bhd and CIMB Investment Bank Bhd are acting as joint principal advisers and joint lead arrangers for the Sukuk Wakalah Programme, as well as joint lead managers for the RM4 billion issuance.

items.title: Kuala Pilah crash: Car skids, plunges onto house roof in Seri Menanti
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, May 29 — A car skidded out of control and ended up on the roof of a house in an unusual late-night accident near Seri Menanti. According to national news agency Bernama, the incident occurred along Jalan Kampung Tanjung Bachang, where the vehicle is believed to have lost control before plunging onto the rooftop of a home situated at the foot of a hill. Kuala Pilah District Police Chief Supt Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said two passengers sustained minor injuries and were treated at Kuala Pilah Hospital.

items.title: Tonton booth draws crowds at PBAKL 2026 with exclusive merchandise, streaming deals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Tonton booth at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair 2026 (PBAKL 2026) is drawing large crowds with its exclusive limited-edition merchandise, special subscription packages, and family-friendly activities. Organised through a collaboration between Tonton and Media Prima Television Networks, the booth at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur (WTCKL) offers visitors special promotional rates available only during the 10-day event. TV3, TV9, and Tonton activation content marketing senior executive Muhammad Khairol Anuar Zawawi said visitors who subscribe to Tonton packages during the fair will receive premium gifts.

items.title: Buffalo attack survivor in Johor recalls terrifying ordeal
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A Felda Ulu Tebrau supervisor sustained severe injuries after being gored and tossed twice by a one-tonne buffalo during a Hari Raya Aidiladha korban programme in Kampung Sungai Tiram, Ulu Tiram, on Wednesday. "I came to watch the slaughter process and did not expect to be gored by the buffalo," he said when met at his home in Batu 20, Kampung Sungai Tiram, today. I fell, and the buffalo gored and tossed me twice," he said.

items.title: Late selling drags Bursa Malaysia to close marginally lower
items.summary: Turnover rose to 4.94 billion units valued at RM10.74 billion compared with 3.53 billion units valued at RM4.19 billion on Thursday. Warrants turnover slipped to 830.47 million units valued at RM105.18 million from 971.39 million units valued at RM117.20 million yesterday. The ACE Market volume narrowed to 550.09 million units valued at RM218.76 million from 583.66 million units valued at RM206.61 million previously.

items.title: U Mobile sets new national standards for indoor connectivity
items.summary: RECENT Opensignal findings showed improvements in U Mobile's indoor 5G connectivity and network consistency following the rollout of its independently operated ULTRA5G network. Over the past year, Opensignal data also recorded a 51 per cent improvement in 5G download speed and a 27 per cent increase in upload speed among U Mobile users. Opensignal's findings showed that U Mobile users were twice as likely to experience an "Excellent" indoor 5G signal, while only eight per cent of indoor readings on U Mobile's network fell into the "Weak", "Very Weak" or "Dead Zone" categories.

items.title: MyIMMs system disruption caused by internal technical issue, no cyberattack involved, says Home Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — The Ministry of Home Affairs (KDN) has assured that the temporary disruption involving the Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs) that affected inspection and processing operations at several entry points in the country today does not involve any cybersecurity incident. KDN in a statement today said the disruption was caused by an internal technical problem that affected the system’s operations for approximately three hours and 45 minutes before immediate restorations were implemented. “KDN would like to emphasise that the disruption that occurred did not involve any cybersecurity incident.

items.title: Ambulance driver charged with murder of girlfriend's 2-year-old son
items.summary: TEMERLOH: A 29-year-old ambulance driver was charged in the magistrate's court here today with the murder of a two-year-old boy on May 11. No plea was recorded from the accused, Muhammad Iqbal Fitri Mazlan, after the charge was read before magistrate Anis Adlina Abu Johan. Iqbal was accused of murdering Mohamad Hayder Abdul Mukmin at a house in Taman Seberang Temerloh Indah 2A about 9am on May 11.

items.title: Police refer DUI case back to prosecution after rider dies in coma
items.summary: KLANG: The police have referred the driving under the influence (DUI) case involving an unemployed man back to the prosecution for review after the food delivery rider he crashed into died following 13 days in a coma. South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey confirmed the matter but said there have been no further developments so far. On May 18, Low Kean Peng, 35, pleaded not guilty to driving under the influence of alcohol and causing serious injury to Zulfiqar.

items.title: Decomposing body found in parked car near USM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A decomposing body, believed to be dead for five days, was found in a car opposite Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) on Jalan Sungai Dua here today. When firefighters forced open the car door, they found the bloated body of a 37-year-old man slumped inside. Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue station operations commander Shahrunnizam Kamar Bashah said firefighters were despatched to the scene after receiving a call at 12.28pm.

items.title: Three Malaysian cadets complete training at elite US military service academies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three Malaysian cadets are among the latest to have completed their studies at the prestigious United States Military Academy at West Point and the United States Naval Academy (USNA), respectively. The US Embassy in Kuala Lumpur, in its social media posts, extended congratulations to Cadet Edlan Mikyad Ahmad Syamly, who graduated from West Point, as well as Cadets Qarin Loqman Safriza and Mohamad Danish Imran Zainudin, who graduated from USNA. Edlan's journey reflects not only his personal grit and dedication but also the deep, enduring defence and educational partnerships between the United States and Malaysia.

items.title: Media a strategic partner in ensuring country's stability, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has conveyed his appreciation to all journalists, media practitioners, and broadcasting personnel for their unwavering dedication to delivering accurate, authentic, and integrity-driven information. In conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 today, he said journalists play a significant role in strengthening democracy and shaping a knowledgeable society, especially in an increasingly challenging and fast-changing media landscape. Fadillah said a free, responsible, and ethical media is a strategic partner of the government in ensuring Malaysia's stability, harmony, and continued progress.

items.title: Subang MP urges PM's Dept to explain blocked access to MyKhas portal
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen has urged the Prime Minister's Department to provide a written explanation for the restriction of access to the MyKhas portal, which has remained unresolved for more than a week. He said that despite multiple official communications, including hand-delivered letters to the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister's Department in Selangor and its headquarters in Putrajaya, no formal explanation had been given and access to the portal had yet to be restored. Asked whether the issue would affect his decision to stay in PKR, Wong said the matter had added pressure to an already difficult decision.

items.title: Anwar voices concern over Gaza, southern Lebanon during Aidiladha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed deep concern over the situation facing Palestinians in Gaza and southern Lebanon as Muslims celebrate Hari Raya Aidiladha. "During this Eid al-Adha celebration, we see Muslims, especially in Gaza and South Lebanon, being subjected to extreme Zionist Israeli oppression. Anwar said he does not condone any harm against Jews, but stressed that the ongoing oppression of Palestinians must be condemned.

items.title: Do not blame mountain guides for hiker's disappearance, says rescued climber
items.summary: Hanafi and Jaslinda were among 14 participants and two MGPs who began the Trans Spencer Chapman trek via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route at 2am on May 23. Jaslinda, meanwhile, remains missing. Recalling Sunday morning, Hanafi said Jaslinda and Apis headed toward the peak of Gunung Batu Putih while he waited at the camp.

items.title: Saifuddin: Technical fault, not cyberattack, caused 3-hour immigration system outage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thousands of travellers were caught in massive queues at immigration checkpoints nationwide after a technical failure crippled the country's immigration system for more than three hours yesterday. The disruption, involving the Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs), affected major entry points across the country, including the heavily congested Johor Causeway and Second Link crossings' land checkpoints during the morning rush into Singapore. "The temporary disruption involving the MyIMMs system has been fully restored.

items.title: Malaya Heritage eyes Nasdaq listing in 2027, US$15mil raise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaya Heritage Holdings Ltd is eyeing a listing on the Nasdaq Stock Market in 2027 and plans to raise US$15 million (RM67.5 million) through the proposed listing to support its domestic and international expansion. Deputy chief executive officer Oscar Woon said the company has been undergoing the Nasdaq listing process for the past six to eight months. Sagtec Global chief executive officer Kevin Ng said Malaya Heritage's Nasdaq listing ambition is aimed at strengthening global brand recognition and promoting Malaysia's kopitiam culture internationally, while showcasing the country's multicultural identity.

items.title: Kluang childcare teacher accused of forcing pupil to drink chilli-infused water
items.summary: KLUANG, May 29 — Police are investigating a claim that a seven-year-old child was forced to drink chilli-infused water by a teacher at a childcare centre in Taman Sri Kluang here. Kluang police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the child's guardian lodged a report at about 8.46 pm yesterday, alleging that the teacher had forced the child to consume the drink. Bahrin said the case is being investigated under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 and urged anyone with information on the incident to contact the nearest police station or the Kluang district police headquarters at 07-7784222.

items.title: Immigration counters nationwide shut until June 3 for Agong’s birthday, Wesak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — All Immigration Department (JIM) counters, including at Urban Transformation Centres (UTCs), will be closed on Monday (June 1) and Tuesday (June 2). It said in a social media post that the counters will be closed on Monday in conjunction with the Official Birthday of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and Wesak Day on Tuesday. Earlier, the government agreed to grant public holidays in conjunction with Wesak Day and the Official Birthday of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.

items.title: Govt opens tenders for battery energy storage projects nationwide to strengthen power grid
items.summary: KUCHING, May 29 — Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) projects are being expanded nationwide as the government moves to strengthen grid stability and support the growing use of renewable energy, particularly solar power. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said tenders for BESS projects have been opened at several locations in peninsular Malaysia following the increasing implementation of renewable energy initiatives such as solar. He said BESS plays a crucial role in maintaining grid stability as solar power depends on sunlight, while renewable energy sources that do not provide base load supply can affect the stability of the electricity system.

items.title: Foul smell leads to grisly discovery of decomposed body in rented Melaka shophouse, tenant’s son in custody as suspect
items.summary: MELAKA, May 29 — A decomposed body, believed to have been dead for between two weeks and a month, was found wrapped in a folded mattress inside a shophouse in Pokok Mangga here last night. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the body, whose identity has yet to be established, was discovered after the owner of the premises inspected the property when he was unable to contact the tenant. Upon entering the shophouse, the owner detected a foul smell and found a suspicious mattress bundle in the living room before alerting the police.

items.title: Private ambulance driver faces murder charge over death of two-year-old in Temerloh
items.summary: TEMERLOH, May 29 — A private ambulance driver was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a two-year-old boy. No plea was recorded from the accused, Muhammad Iqbal Fitri Mazlan, 29, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The charge was read out before Magistrate Anis Adlina Abu Johan.

items.title: Man charged in Sandakan with trafficking Malaysian teen and three foreigners for sex work
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 – A 32-year-old man was charged in the Sandakan Magistrate’s Court today with trafficking four people, including three minor girls, for sexual exploitation. Chu faces three charges of trafficking minors for sexual exploitation, involving two 15-year-olds—one a Malaysian and the other a foreigner—and a 17-year-old foreigner. The court denied bail for the accused and set June 15 for mention at the Sessions Court.

items.title: Anwar says will clarify MA63 negotiations and grant review in Sabah tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he will provide clarification on negotiations between the Federal Government and the Sabah government, including the review of Sabah’s 40 per cent special grant entitlement under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), tomorrow. “I will explain that tomorrow in Sabah. Yesterday, Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the federal government remains committed to correcting the previous administration’s official position, including undertaking a review of Sabah’s 40 per cent special grant entitlement.

items.title: ‘This is dangerous’: Anwar warns Malaysians against mistaking social media snippets for knowledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that children are losing the habit of reading as social media shortens attention spans and replaces knowledge with bite-sized information. Launching the 2026 National Book Fair at the World Trade Centre here, Anwar who professed himself a bookworm, urged schools, teachers and parents to reignite interest in reading to build a more informed society. “The reading culture has become a problem in the modern world,” he said.

items.title: Storm warning out for Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan, Johor, Sarawak, Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for seven states today, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 7.00 pm. Central and southern states are also affected, specifically Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin in Negeri Sembilan, alongside Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, and Kota Tinggi in Johor. In East Malaysia, MetMalaysia has flagged several divisions in Sarawak, namely Sibu, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau), and Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri, and Marudi).

items.title: Missing witness sinks case against two men accused of possessing 150kg of drugs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 29 — Two former steamboat restaurant workers were acquitted and freed by the High Court here today after a judge ruled the prosecution failed to secure a key witness sought by the defence in a case involving nearly 150kg of methamphetamine and heroin. Chan Shi Wei, 29, and Lim Chan Hau, 30, had spent six years in remand over the charges linked to a house in Kota Damansara. Bhupindar also found the prosecution failed to effectively challenge key aspects of the defence during cross-examination.

items.title: RM24m Bukit Jalil stadium upgrade project under MACC scrutiny after complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today confirmed it is reviewing complaints linked to the evaluation process and upgrade works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil. It said it is examining related information and documents before deciding on further investigation, Berita Harian reported this afternoon. “MACC always takes seriously any allegations involving corruption, abuse of power and misconduct affecting public interest and government procurement,” it was quoted as saying.

items.title: Son of Taiping Prison inmate Gan Chin Eng says ‘no closure’ despite Suhakam inquiry findings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The son of inmate Gan Chin Eng, who died in Taiping Prison in January last year, says he has yet to find closure after the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) released its findings on the case. Suhakam’s public inquiry reported evidence of abuse and negligence, pointing to systemic shortcomings in the prison’s management and administration. Gan, 62, died following the incident.

items.title: Video of Perlis MB shooting sacrificial cow sparks police probe
items.summary: KANGAR, May 29 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah is set to be questioned by police after a video clip showed him shooting a cow with a rifle during an Aidiladha sacrifice ceremony yesterday. Abu Bakar admitted to shooting the cow but said did it to prevent anyone from getting hurt after the animal had broken loose. Kangar police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said investigations have started, Berita Harian reported this afternoon.

items.title: Ahead of official birthday, Friday sermon highlights King’s role as active guardian of stability and sovereignty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Yang di-Pertuan Agong not only symbolises the sovereignty of the nation, but is also a guardian who protects the citizens, safeguards the religion and is the mainstay of the nation's stability. “Indeed, religious and royal institutions form the key foundations that preserve the nation's sovereignty, the people's unity and the sanctity of the religion of Islam in this blessed nation. If leaders protect the nation, citizens should preserve the nation's soul,” the sermon read.

items.title: Anwar says budget cuts to Education Ministry unavoidable but manageable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he saw no reason why the Ministry of Education cannot manage the impending cuts of its RM20 billion annual budget, as his government is forced to slash spending to cope with the economic fallout from war in the Middle East. “You can appeal as much as you want but the cuts will still happen,” Anwar told top officials of the ministry who attended the 2026 National Book Fair here. The budget cuts drew some criticism, but Anwar said the cuts will only affect “non-critical” spending.

items.title: NRD corrects ‘Islam’ status on Christian woman’s MyPR card — with the exact same mistake
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — An Indonesian Christian woman today came to the National Registration Department (NRD) headquarters in Putrajaya with the hope of receiving a new permanent resident identification card (MyPR) that would correctly reflect her religious status as a non-Muslim. The 68-year-old woman, who prefers to be known as Yanti* and is the widow of a Malaysian man, was previously given a MyPR card with the word “Islam” printed. But Yanti was left disappointed after coming all the way from Johor by car today, as the NRD issued her a new MyPR card with the exact same error as her old MyPR card.

items.title: Najib files for new evidence in bid to overturn 1MDB conviction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has filed an application to introduce fresh evidence before the Court of Appeal for his bid to overturn his conviction on all 25 charges of abuse of power and money laundering linked to the 1MDB scandal. In a legal context, “fresh evidence” refers to information discovered after a trial concludes. Najib is seeking to introduce evidence concerning fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, and former 1MDB legal counsel Jasmine Loo into his appeal hearing.

items.title: Finance Ministry warns of jail time, heavy fine over BUDI95 MyKad identity theft
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Ministry of Finance has warned of a RM20,000 fine and three years’ jail for anyone caught misusing another person’s MyKad to claim BUDI MADANI RON95 (BUDI95) fuel subsidies, following a spike in identity theft reports at petrol stations. The MoF said it has also directed all oil retail companies to immediately tighten compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs) for MyKad verification. “Any transaction carried out manually without prior physical MyKad verification is not permitted, as it creates room for unauthorised transactions, subsidy abuse, and violations of the programme's integrity,” the ministry said.

items.title: IJN cardiothoracic surgeon is first Malaysian elected to prestigious American association
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — For more than three decades, Prof Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Jeswant Dillon has spent countless hours in operating theatres handling some of Malaysia's most complex cardiothoracic surgery cases, while balancing the demands of clinical practice, academia, research and mentorship. Earlier this month, the Senior Consultant Cardiothoracic Surgeon at Institut Jantung Negara (IJN) added another milestone to his distinguished career when he became the first Malaysian elected as a member of the prestigious American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS), placing Malaysia on the global cardiothoracic surgery map. For Dr Jeswant, the recognition represents more than a professional accolade, reflecting his unwavering commitment and dedication to advancing cardiothoracic surgery both in Malaysia and internationally.

items.title: Loke says independent task force to probe Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment; services to resume June 3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The train that derailed at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station yesterday will be hoisted and removed today, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced. Conducting a site inspection this morning, Loke told the press that a thorough investigation of the track condition will be carried out by an independent special task force and the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD). “This morning, I visited the site with the special task force and APAD to see for myself the condition of the track that caused the derailment yesterday.

items.title: Storm blows off roofs of nine houses and surau in Kijal, no injuries reported
items.summary: CHUKAI, May 29 — Families rushed to safety after strong winds and heavy rain damaged nine houses and a surau in Kampung Yak Yah, Ibok, Kijal near here yesterday evening. Kijal assemblyman Datuk Razali Idris said the storm struck at about 4.30 pm, with most of the houses suffering severe roof damage while two homes were damaged after being struck by fallen trees. He said they were shocked when a durian tree suddenly toppled and crashed onto the house.

items.title: Malaysia’s No. 1 Islamic economy title an achievement regardless of race or religion, says minister Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — Malaysia has retained its position as the world’s leading Islamic economy in 2025, a recognition that highlights the country’s strong Islamic financial system, halal industry, innovation and development ecosystem. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the achievement, based on the State of the Global Islamic Economy (SGIE) Report 2024/2025, reflected international confidence in Malaysia’s political stability, clear economic policies and overall development direction. He said the ranking demonstrated that the Madani Government’s emphasis on good governance, investor confidence and inclusive development continued to receive recognition at the global level.

items.title: ‘Mysterious’ one-day Johor assembly sitting on June 22 sparks fresh dissolution rumour
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 29 — The sudden calling of a "mysterious" one-day special sitting of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 22 has reignited intense speculation over the potential dissolution of the state government. Johor Jaya assemblyman Liow Cai Tung disclosed today that she had received a formal notice for the session, noting several "unprecedented" anomalies in the scheduling. “If it's not to dissolve the Johor State Legislative Assembly, then what is the agenda for this session?
